g.data-narration: Linear regression is a supervised learning algorithm that models the relationship between input features and a continuous target variable.
g.text: A supervised learning algorithm that models the relationship between
dependent (target) and independent (feature) variables by fitting a

g.data-narration: Simple linear regression uses one feature to predict the target, following the equation y equals wx plus b, where w is weight and b is bias.
g.text: Simple Linear Regression: y = wx + b
• w: Weight (slope) - how much y changes per unit change in x
• b: Bias (intercept) - value of y when x = 0

g.data-narration: Multiple linear regression extends to many features using vector notation, where we compute the dot product of weights and features plus bias.
g.text: Extends to multiple features: y = w₁x₁ + w₂x₂ + ... + wₙxₙ + b
In vector form: y = wᵀx + b where w = [w₁, w₂, ..., wₙ]ᵀ

g.data-narration: Linear regression requires five key assumptions: linearity, independence, homoscedasticity, normality, and no multicollinearity.
The cost function measures error using Mean Squared Error, which averages the squared differences between actual and predicted values.
g.text: The model minimizes the average squared difference between predictions and actual values:

g.data-narration: Gradient descent iteratively updates weights by moving in the opposite direction of the gradient, using a learning rate to control step size.
g.text: Iteratively updates weights to minimize MSE:

g.data-narration: The normal equation provides a closed-form solution to find optimal weights directly without iterative optimization.
g.text: Small n, many features → Gradient Descent
Many n, few features → Normal Eq

g.data-narration: Ridge regression adds L2 penalty to shrink coefficients, while Lasso uses L1 penalty which can zero out features for selection.
g.text: Shrinks toward zero, never exactly zero
Can set coefficients exactly to zero

g.data-narration: Key evaluation metrics include MSE, RMSE, MAE, and R-squared which measures the proportion of variance explained by the model.
Gradient descent has time complexity O(n × d × iterations), while the normal equation is O(n × d² plus d cubed).
Linear regression is used for house price prediction, sales forecasting, stock trends, medical dosage prediction, and energy estimation.
In summary, linear regression fits a line to minimize squared errors, offering interpretability and serving as a foundation for more complex models.
g.text: • y = wx + b is the core equation | MSE is the cost function | Ridge/Lasso prevent overfitting
• Gradient Descent for large datasets | Normal Equation for small datasets
